summary: Solid TDD cycle with good behavioral test coverage. The critical issue is that close() does not settle the tokenPromise — callers who await tokenPromise after calling close() will hang forever. The timeout cleanup also has a subtle gap where the promise can leak.

suggestions.description: close() does not reject tokenPromise, causing callers to hang
suggestions.rationale: If a caller calls close() before a token arrives or timeout fires, the tokenPromise remains forever pending. Any code that awaits tokenPromise after close() will hang indefinitely. This is a real bug for the use case where a user cancels the auth flow.
suggestions.suggestion: In the close() function, check if \!settled and if so, set settled = true and call rejectToken(new Error("Auth server closed")) before closing the server. Add a test: call close() without sending any request, then expect tokenPromise to reject.

suggestions.description: No test for empty token query param (?token=)
suggestions.rationale: The implementation treats empty string as missing (falsy check on line 40), returning 400. This is correct behavior but is not tested. An empty token= param is a realistic edge case from malformed OAuth redirects.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: fetch /callback?token= and assert 400 status.

suggestions.description: Response body text is not asserted
suggestions.rationale: The implementation returns a specific success message (line 47) but no test verifies the response body content. If someone changes or removes this text, no test catches it.
suggestions.suggestion: In the token callback test, add: const body = await response.text(); expect(body).toContain("successful") or similar.

suggestions.description: Comment about server.close() behavior is misleading
suggestions.rationale: The comment says the callback fires immediately if the server is already closed. In reality, server.close() passes an ERR_SERVER_NOT_RUNNING error to the callback when called on an already-closed server. The code works because it ignores the error parameter, but the comment misrepresents the mechanism.
suggestions.suggestion: Update the comment to: // If server is already closed, callback receives ERR_SERVER_NOT_RUNNING which we ignore
